description: Test cases for the evaluation-framework skill. Covers diverse input types, domains, and user phrasings.

evals.name: startup-business-plan-text
evals.prompt: I have a startup idea I want to fully evaluate before pitching investors. We're building an AI-powered legal document review platform targeting small law firms in Southeast Asia. The market is fragmented, no one dominant player yet. We plan to charge $500/month per firm. We have a team of 4: 2 engineers, 1 lawyer, 1 business dev. We've spoken with 12 law firms and 9 said they'd pay for this. We're looking to raise $800K seed round. Can you give me a comprehensive evaluation of this?
evals.expected_output: Comprehensive evaluation report using Lean Canvas, SWOT, Porter's Five Forces, NPV/ROI, and Risk Matrix at minimum. Should identify non-obvious risks (e.g., regulatory compliance per country, data sovereignty) and opportunities (regional market consolidation). Report should have all 6 sections populated.

assertions.text: Report includes Executive Summary section
assertions.check: response contains '## Executive Summary' or '# Executive Summary'

assertions.text: At least 3 different named frameworks are applied
assertions.check: response contains framework names (SWOT, Porter, Lean Canvas, Risk Matrix, NPV, etc.)

assertions.text: Financial viability is assessed (ROI/NPV mentioned)
assertions.check: response contains 'ROI' or 'NPV' or 'payback' or 'financial' section

assertions.text: At least one non-obvious insight is surfaced
assertions.check: response contains regulatory, data sovereignty, localization, or market fragmentation insight

assertions.text: Recommended Next Steps section is present with specific actions
assertions.check: response contains 'Next Steps' or 'Recommendations' with numbered/bulleted actions

evals.name: tech-architecture-migration
evals.prompt: My company needs to migrate from a monolithic Java application (12 years old, ~800K lines of code) to a microservices architecture on AWS. We have 50 engineers, 18-month timeline, $3.2M budget. The app handles financial transactions — about 2 million per day. We have zero downtime requirements and SOC2 Type II compliance obligations. Please evaluate this migration plan comprehensively.
evals.expected_output: Should apply TOGAF ADM, DevSecOps Maturity, Risk Matrix, and potentially McKinsey 7S for the org change component. Must flag zero-downtime as high complexity, SOC2 as a critical constraint, and financial transaction data as a critical risk area. Financial assessment of $3.2M budget.

assertions.text: Technology domain frameworks are selected (TOGAF, DevSecOps, or equivalent)
assertions.check: response contains 'TOGAF' or 'DevSecOps' or 'architecture' or 'maturity model'

assertions.text: Risk Matrix is applied
assertions.check: response contains 'Risk Matrix' or 'risk assessment' with likelihood and impact

assertions.text: SOC2/compliance is flagged as critical constraint
assertions.check: response mentions 'SOC2' or 'compliance' as risk or constraint

assertions.text: Zero-downtime requirement is analyzed
assertions.check: response mentions 'zero downtime' or 'blue-green' or 'canary' or 'migration strategy'

assertions.text: Priority Matrix or Recommended Actions present
assertions.check: response contains 'Priority Matrix' or 'Next Steps' or 'Recommended Actions'

evals.name: policy-program-evaluation
evals.prompt: We're a government agency launching a $5M digital literacy program for rural communities in Vietnam. The program will train 10,000 farmers over 2 years to use mobile banking and e-commerce platforms. We have 15 provincial coordinators and partnerships with 3 telecoms. Success will be measured by how many participants complete the training and start transacting online within 6 months. Please build us a full evaluation framework.
evals.expected_output: Should apply Theory of Change (primary), Kirkpatrick Model, Cost-Benefit Analysis, and Risk Matrix. Theory of Change should map the full input→impact chain. Should flag assumption gaps (connectivity in rural areas, smartphone ownership rates). Should identify Kirkpatrick Level 3 (behavior change) as the key measurement challenge.

assertions.text: Theory of Change framework is applied
assertions.check: response contains 'Theory of Change' or 'logic chain' or 'inputs → activities → outputs'

assertions.text: Kirkpatrick Model is applied or recommended
assertions.check: response contains 'Kirkpatrick' or 'Level 1/2/3/4' or 'reaction, learning, behavior, results'

assertions.text: Key assumptions are identified
assertions.check: response mentions 'assumption' at least twice with specific examples

assertions.text: Financial/cost analysis is included
assertions.check: response mentions cost per participant, cost-benefit, or budget analysis

assertions.text: Non-obvious insight surfaced (connectivity, smartphone access, or literacy prerequisite)
assertions.check: response mentions connectivity, smartphone ownership, prerequisite literacy, or digital divide

evals.name: vague-input-clarification
evals.prompt: I have a new project idea I want to evaluate. Can you help?
evals.expected_output: Skill should enter clarification mode. Should ask 2-3 targeted questions (goal, stakeholders, constraints) rather than proceeding with a generic framework. Should NOT produce a full evaluation report without more information.

assertions.text: Skill asks clarifying questions instead of producing a full report
assertions.check: response contains a question mark AND does not contain 'Executive Summary'

assertions.text: At least 2 specific questions are asked
assertions.check: response contains at least 2 question marks in question sentences

assertions.text: Questions address goal, stakeholders, or constraints
assertions.check: response contains 'goal' or 'objective' or 'stakeholder' or 'constraint' in question context

evals.name: product-feature-requirement
evals.prompt: Our product team wants to add an AI-powered chatbot to our SaaS HR platform. The chatbot should handle employee onboarding questions, leave requests, and payroll queries. We serve 200 mid-size companies (500-5000 employees). Dev team is 8 engineers. Timeline: 6 months. Budget: $400K. We already have GPT-4 API access. Our NPS is currently 42. We want to increase it by 15 points post-launch. Please evaluate this feature requirement.
evals.expected_output: Should apply JTBD, Kano Model, OKR Alignment Audit, Risk Matrix, and NPV/ROI. Should flag that NPS +15 target needs validation against user research. Should identify JTBD for 'why do employees contact HR at all?' as key upstream question.

assertions.text: Product domain frameworks applied (JTBD, Kano, or Design Thinking)
assertions.check: response contains 'Jobs-To-Be-Done' or 'JTBD' or 'Kano' or 'Design Thinking'

assertions.text: OKR or success criteria quality is assessed
assertions.check: response mentions 'OKR' or 'NPS target' or 'success criteria' or 'measurable'

assertions.text: Risk Matrix or risk analysis is present
assertions.check: response contains 'risk' in structured analysis context

assertions.text: Financial or ROI analysis covers the $400K budget
assertions.check: response mentions '$400' or 'ROI' or 'cost per' in financial context

assertions.text: Score table or Priority Matrix is present
assertions.check: response contains a markdown table with scores or a Priority Matrix section
